title: Error Handling
description: ErrorBoundary and catchError catch errors in reactive computations and child components.
error_boundary_intro: <0>ErrorBoundary</0> catches errors thrown in its children and renders a fallback:
catch_error_intro: For lower-level control, <0>catchError</0> wraps a function and catches errors in child computations:
error_propagation_body: Errors propagate up the owner chain. If no error boundary catches an error, it bubbles to the root and throws. Nested error boundaries catch errors from their subtree only.
handler_errors_intro: <0>useAsyncOutput</0> handlers run in the runtime's async deployment loop, not inside reactive computations. Errors thrown in handlers cause the deployment to fail. They are <1>not</1> caught by <2>ErrorBoundary</2>. Handle errors inside your handler with try/catch:
warning_cleanup: Cleanup functions registered with <0>onCleanup</0> still run when an error boundary catches an error. Resources are released.
